headline: 'Extreme Measures Of Sentiment': Stock Market Update For The Week Ahead
summary:
The Past Week, In A Nutshell

What Happened: In an extension of the sharp rotation out of safety on a sustained economic rebound after the COVID-19 coronavirus pandemic, stretched sentiment propelled stocks to record closing highs.

headline: Wall Street Braces As Tesla Addition to S&P 500 Could Put $100 Billion Into Play: WSJ
summary: Wall Street is bracing for Tesla Inc's (NASDAQ: TSLA) arrival to the S&P 500 Index on Dec. 18.

headline: Guggenheim Fund Mulls Investment in Grayscale Bitcoin Trust
summary: Guggenheim Strategic Opportunities Fund (NYSE: GOF) is considering gaining some “investment exposure" to Bitcoin, a filing shows.

headline: First Made-In-China Tesla Model 3s Reportedly Begin Arriving In Europe
summary: As Tesla Inc (NASDAQ: TSLA) grows in popularity, it is building factories across the world to meet demand.

headline: 40% Of Tesla's Sales In 2022 Could Be In China: Wedbush Securities
summary:
Wedbush Securities has issued another bullish report on electric vehicles and Tesla Inc (NASDAQ: TSLA).

Growth Markets: The report makes the case for believing heavy growth is to come from Europe, the U.S. and, especially, China.

headline: Tesla To Spend $6.4M On New Supercharger Factory In China
summary: Tesla Inc (NASDAQ: TSLA) is investing in another factory to build one of its most important products.

headline: The Week Ahead In Biotech: Hematology Conference Gets Underway, Vanda And BioCryst Await FDA Decisions
summary: Biotech stocks had a rather uneventful week, as the sector was seen mostly flatlining amid light trading volume ahead of the Thanksgiving holiday.
